title: Acyclicity

lean_statement.name: IsAcyclic
lean_statement.code:
/-- The CDMG `G` is *acyclic*: no node admits a non-trivial directed
walk to itself. Mirrors `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` def
3.6 (`\label{def-acylic}`) verbatim, with "non-trivial" read as
`1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length` per `Walks.lean`'s `Walk.length` doc. The trivial walk
`Walk.nil v` is excluded by the `1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length` conjunct (it has
length zero), so a witness against acyclicity is genuinely a
directed cycle through `v` of one or more steps. Used as the
precondition for claim_3_2 (`AcyclicIffTopologicalOrder`), def_3_7
(graph type names), and pervasively in chapters 4 -- 16. -/
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG ╬▒) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, ┬¼ Ōłā ŽĆ : Walk G v v, ŽĆ.IsDirected Ōł¦ 1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length

lean_explanation:
The predicate fixes an ambient context: `╬▒ : Type*` is the vertex type and `G : CDMG ╬▒` is the conditional directed mixed graph under discussion, matching the LN's preamble "$G = (J, V, E, L)$". The universally bound `v` ranges over vertices of `G`, and inside the negated existential `ŽĆ : Walk G v v` is a walk that starts and ends at the same node `v` ŌĆö exactly the LN's "walk from $v$ to itself".

Two Mathlib idioms appear. First, `ŌłĆ v Ōłł G, _` is sugar for `ŌłĆ v, v Ōłł G ŌåÆ _`, where `v Ōłł G` uses the `Membership ╬▒ (CDMG ╬▒)` instance from def_3_2 that unfolds (definitionally) to `v Ōłł G.J Ōł¬ G.V`; this matches the LN's literal "for any node $v \in G$". Second, the predicate lives in `Prop` and is registered under the `CDMG` namespace, so callers write `G.IsAcyclic` via dot-projection ŌĆö reading just like the LN's prose "the CDMG $G$ is acyclic".

The shape is a universally quantified negated existential. The outer quantifier picks an arbitrary vertex `v` of `G`. The inner `┬¼ Ōłā ŽĆ : Walk G v v, ŽĆ.IsDirected Ōł¦ 1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length` denies that any walk from $v$ back to $v$ is both directed (every step `forward`, matching the LN's $\tuh$) and non-trivial (length at least one). The trivial walk `Walk.nil v : Walk G v v` always exists but has `length = 0`, so the `1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length` conjunct rules it out ŌĆö what's forbidden is a genuine directed cycle through `v` of one or more steps. The two ingredients `Walk.IsDirected` and `Walk.length` are imported verbatim from def_3_4, so the whole predicate is a one-line composition on top of the walk data layer.
design_choices:
**Dot-projection `G.IsAcyclic`, namespaced under `Causality.CDMG`.** The LN's prose treats acyclicity as a property of the graph ŌĆö "the CDMG $G$ is acyclic", "if $G$ is acyclic then ...", "$G$ is acyclic iff ...". Placing the predicate inside the `CDMG` namespace lets downstream callers write `G.IsAcyclic`, which reads exactly like the LN. The same convention is used by every other CDMG-level operator in this folder (`CDMG.Adjacent`, `CDMG.EdgeInto`, the arrow notations `Ō¤Č[G]` / `Ō¤Ę[G]`, and the family operators of def_3_5). The alternative ŌĆö a top-level `Causality.IsAcyclic G` ŌĆö would be slightly more compact but would disconnect acyclicity from the CDMG family of operators and read less like the LN's "$G$ is ..." prose.

**The outer quantifier writes `v Ōłł G`, not `v Ōłł G.J Ōł¬ G.V`.** Both unfold to the same proposition via the `Membership ╬▒ (CDMG ╬▒)` instance from def_3_2 (`CDMG.mem_iff` is `Iff.rfl`). We pick the `v Ōłł G` form because the LN writes "for any node $v \in G$" literally, and the project's convention ŌĆö used uniformly across the family operators in `FamilyDirect.lean` ŌĆö is to mirror that prose. Callers who need to peel `v Ōłł G` back to a set-theoretic union reach for `simp [CDMG.mem_iff]` or `Set.mem_union`.

**"Non-trivial" is encoded as `1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length`, not `ŽĆ ŌēĀ Walk.nil v`.** All three of `1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length`, `ŽĆ.length ŌēĀ 0`, and `ŽĆ ŌēĀ Walk.nil v` are propositionally equivalent once the endpoints `v = v` are pinned down, but the `Nat`-level form is the LN's own framing ŌĆö def_3_4 parameterises walks by length $n \geq 0$, and `Walks.lean`'s docstring already commits to "non-trivial directed walk means length $\geq 1$". Choosing `1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length` also keeps the predicate compositional with later length-based arguments: claim_3_2's `ŌåÆ` direction inducts on walk length, and several later chapters compare `ŽĆ.length` against `ŽĆ.support.length`. The disequality form `ŽĆ ŌēĀ Walk.nil v` would force an awkward `Walk.nil` case-split at every use site, and for general walks `ŽĆ : Walk G v w` with `v ŌēĀ w` it is not even structurally equivalent to length-zero ŌĆö the `Nat`-level form sidesteps that bookkeeping.

**`Prop`, not `Type _` ŌĆö no witness data carried by `IsAcyclic`.** The LN uses acyclicity only as a precondition ŌĆö "if $G$ is acyclic, then ..." ŌĆö never as data carrying constructive information. A `Type _`-valued formulation would force every later "$G$ is acyclic" hypothesis to drag an explicit witness around, cluttering signatures with no proof-theoretic gain (the predicate is decidable only in restricted finite settings, so constructive content would not be useful in general). `Prop` also matches Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.IsAcyclic` precedent.

**Inline conjunction, no helper `IsNonTrivialDirectedCycle`.** The inner `ŽĆ.IsDirected Ōł¦ 1 Ōēż ŽĆ.length` is used in only this one place and reads cleanly inline; naming an alias would add an extra layer of unfolding for downstream `simp`/`rewrite` steps without compensating readability gains. If a later row repeatedly references the same conjunction (claim_3_2 might want to name "directed cycle through $v$"), that row can introduce the helper locally where it pays for itself.

**No bundled simp characterisation lemma.** The defining body is already the shape downstream callers want to reason against; an `isAcyclic_iff` lemma would just be `Iff.rfl` and offer no rewriting power. The negation form $\neg G.\text{IsAcyclic} \iff \exists v \in G,\ \exists \pi : \text{Walk}\ G\ v\ v,\ \pi.\text{IsDirected} \wedge 1 \le \pi.\text{length}$ (via `push_neg`) is a one-line derivation any caller can do, so we leave it for the row that first needs it ŌĆö most likely claim_3_2's `ŌåÉ` direction ŌĆö rather than pre-committing this file to a choice between bundled-`Exists` and unbundled-`bex` shapes.
